<!-- Generated by sync_to_codex.py. Do not edit directly. Source: .claude/commands/do-all-tasks.md. -->
## Codex runtime adapter

- Read `.codex/runtime-contract.md` before delegating or supervising parallel work.
- The native delegation shape is `spawn_agent({task_name, message, fork_turns, agent_type?, model?, reasoning_effort?})`. `task_name` is only a scheduling label and does not bind a custom role.
- Runtime-managed/fixed roles pass `agent_type="<exact-role>"` and omit `model` and `reasoning_effort`. A generic/custom/default selectable worker uses `agent_type="default"` (or the runtime default) and may pass model/effort only when the active schema supports those selectors.
- Use `fork_turns="none"` by default. Pass only a small number of turns when the subtask genuinely needs recent conversation context. Use model/effort selectors only with `fork_turns="none"` or a bounded recent-turn count and only after the active schema accepts those fields.
- Supervise with `followup_task`, `send_message`, `wait_agent`, `list_agents`, and `interrupt_agent`. The parent owns state, checkpoints, aggregation, and conflicts.
- Lifecycle approval and owner authority never replace a native Codex tool request: the needing agent requests the exact command and justification. Chat approval is never a substitute; never request a magic authorization phrase.
- An ordinary low-risk coder or read-only reviewer uses one single-turn spawn with no authorization handshake and no filesystem receipt. Use the handshake only for privileged/high-risk, destructive, deploy, migration, secret-access, or high-risk lifecycle-gate-producing roles that support follow-up; the parent writes the receipt.
- One generic role-bound fallback is allowed only after the native fixed-role spawn returns an explicit unsupported-model error. Missing or unverified advisory metadata never qualifies. Preserve the same canonical role source and same bounded envelope.
- A successful selectable-worker spawn proves requested-selector acceptance, not an independent resolved-model trace. Record the requested selector and acceptance/failure; record a resolved trace only if the runtime exposes one.
- Codex has no automatic cross-session agent memory. When relevant, the parent task envelope explicitly tells the child to read `.claude/agent-memory/{agent}/MEMORY.md` as read-only input; do not claim hidden persistence or shared memory.
- After denied native permission, stop and return control; only new user instruction or a materially changed exact command/justification permits another native request. Automatic or goal-continuation turns must never reissue permission cards.
- Do not promise background callbacks or shared team memory. Children must not create children.
- Keep `.claude/**` as the editable source of truth. Read generated `.agents/**` and `.codex/**` for runtime discovery, but never edit it directly.
- After changing `CLAUDE.md` or `.claude/**`, run `python .claude/shared/scripts/sync_to_codex.py --project . --apply --prune`.


# Command workflow: /do-all-tasks

Treat Claude-only command syntax as workflow intent and use the available Codex tools.

# Do All Tasks

Execute all approved tasks of a feature through parent-orchestrated waves. Read and follow `.claude/shared/pipeline-contract.md`; runtime messages and checkpoints cannot weaken its durable gates.

## Step 1: Load Skill

Invoke Skill tool: `Skill(skill: "feature-execution")`

## Step 2: Find Feature

1. User provides feature path or name
2. Verify `user-spec.md` and `tech-spec.md` both record `status: approved`.
3. Require `tasks-manifest.yml` with `status: approved`. Then verify every listed task exists and dependencies are valid.
4. Require `task-files-map.yml`, verify it covers every declared write, and inventory file overlap.
5. If any gate or durable evidence is missing, malformed, stale, or contradictory, fail closed before mutation and report what is needed.

## Step 3: Plan and Approval

The parent builds a bounded execution plan from the approved manifest and file map. It must serialize tasks with file overlap, schedule only disjoint work in parallel, respect available slots (the parent and every running worker count as an active agent), and enforce no nested fan-out. Show the plan to the owner and wait for explicit approval before starting workers. Only after explicit user approval may execution begin.

After that durable approval exists, apply
`.claude/shared/pipeline-contract.md#post-approval-changes`
only when an approved full-path task or plan artifact changes. An ordinary
technical repair that preserves the approved objective and acceptance remains
inside its task under the existing approval and re-runs only affected targeted
checks — no reapproval needed. Product/authority changes stop for
the owner before mutation.

## Step 4: Execute and Record Durable State

Follow the loaded feature-execution skill workflow.
The parent owns waves, scheduling, aggregation, checkpoint/resume decisions, and shared
durable state. A task completes only when its required tests and reviews pass and the
parent records `done` in the task's frontmatter and in `checkpoint.yml`. A commit,
message, or chat progress is not terminal proof by itself.

**Completion gate:**
After every task passes and feature verification succeeds, the parent atomically writes `work/{feature}/feature-status.yml`. Report completion only when it records `status: complete` with QA passed, zero unresolved findings, and post-deploy passed or explicitly waived when applicable. Otherwise preserve and report the last valid durable state.
